description_long: This object is a vintage cathode-ray tube (CRT) television with a rectangular wooden cabinet and a prominent convex glass screen. The front features a thick, metallic bezel framing the screen, with a vertical speaker grille and a cluster of rotary control knobs on the right side. The wood grain finish covers the top and sides, giving it a classic, mid-20th-century appearance. The back is composed of a large, protruding black plastic housing with ventilation slats and a central access panel, typical of older television designs. The control panel includes several dials and buttons, likely for power, volume, channel selection, and tuning. The overall build is boxy and robust, with a substantial depth due to the CRT technology.
description: A vintage CRT television with a wood-grain cabinet, convex glass screen, metallic bezel, and side control knobs.
description_view_0: Front view showing the convex glass screen, metallic bezel, speaker grille, and a cluster of control knobs on the right.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the wood-grain finish and the deep, boxy profile of the television.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing the large, black plastic housing with ventilation slats and a central access panel.
description_view_3: Right side view highlighting the wood-grain finish and the protruding back of the television.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick metallic bezel, silver-gray color, rectangular with rounded corners, matte finish
attribute_control_button_layout: Cluster of rotary knobs and buttons on the front right side
